aiPrompt: Every time you do an action, you must greet Elena.
aiReasoning:
**Name** — `greet-elena` is kebab-case, descriptive, and immediately conveys the skill's single purpose.

**Description** — Written in third-person ("This skill should be used..."), lists concrete trigger phrases ("create X", "fix Y", "run Z", etc.), and includes the explicit activation phrase ("Make sure to use this skill before every single action..."). Because the trigger is "every action," the description is intentionally pushy and broad to combat undertriggering — exactly what Skill Studio guidance recommends.

**Model / allowedTools** — Left empty. The skill is a behavioral wrapper that applies on top of whatever tools the underlying task needs; restricting tools or forcing a model tier would be counterproductive.

**Body style** — Imperative/infinitive ("Execute a greeting", "Output a greeting", "Proceed with the action"), not second-person. Opens with an execution directive as recommended for action-style skills. Uses ## sections (Purpose, Definition, Workflow, Greeting Format, Rules, Output Format, Why This Matters) for progressive disclosure. Includes concrete examples of acceptable greetings and a sample output flow so another model instance can replicate the behavior consistently. Explains the WHY (drift defeats the purpose) rather than relying on rigid MUST/NEVER alone. Length sits in the 500–800 word target range.

**Edge case handled** — Clarified that pure conversational replies with no action don't require a greeting, preventing absurd over-application while still enforcing the rule on every actual action.

**suggestedPlugin** — `personal` fits best from the existing list since this is a personal preference/courtesy behavior rather than a domain-technical skill.
